tasks.reason: Every executable request must become a traceable Change node before AI execution.
validationRules: Change must derive from exactly one Request
Change must include a non-empty goal

tasks.reason: Change type selects pack schema, check policy, evidence policy, and risk policy.
validationRules: Low confidence blocks AI execution
Conflicting types require HumanDecision

tasks.reason: AI must receive a bounded subgraph, not freely chosen context or the full graph.
validationRules: Missing required node blocks contract generation
Critical Unknown blocks execution

tasks.reason: Allowed and forbidden scope directly control what AI may change.
validationRules: Empty allowedScope blocks execution
Forbidden scope changes block acceptance

tasks.reason: Execution contracts must be compiled from graph and policy facts rather than AI prose.
validationRules: Contract must include goal, scope, checks, evidence, and stop conditions
Invalid contract blocks AI executor

tasks.reason: Required checks are completion conditions and cannot be downgraded by AI.
validationRules: Code changes require at least one Check
Protocol or driver changes need stronger checks

tasks.reason: Evidence is the proof of completion and freshness must be computed from digests.
validationRules: Completed Check requires Evidence
Stale Evidence cannot support acceptance

tasks.reason: AI may phrase a human decision request after compiler has fixed the options and safe default.
validationRules: Question text must not change options
Question text must not reduce risk severity

tasks.reason: AI may make compiler facts readable, but cannot add or override facts.
validationRules: Narrative must not conflict with Fact Block
Failed checks must not be described as pass

tasks.reason: AI may suggest extra tests, but CheckPolicy decides whether any suggestion becomes required.
validationRules: Suggestion is optional until CheckCompiler promotes it
AI cannot mark required checks optional
